title: Wave Problem Generator API
description: Generate physics wave-superposition problem images programmatically. POST a problem spec to /api/generate; receive PNG file paths (or inline dataURLs).

endpoints.POST /api/generate: Generate problem images for one problem spec
endpoints.GET /api/output/:session/:file: Serve a generated PNG by session+filename

wave.vertices: Array of {x:int, y:multiple-of-0.5}. Sorted by x.
wave.speed: Number, propagation speed (cells/sec). Distractor waves typically use 0 (static).
wave.label: Optional label string ('A', 'B', etc.)

cellSize.description: Optional fixed cell pixel size. null = auto. Range: 15..120.
cellSize.type3-note: For type 3 only y-axis cellSize.h is used; the y-t graph width is fixed at 580px.

2.name: Displacement at point and time (numeric answer)

3.name: y-t graph at specific x location
3.distractor-shape: Wave with vertices in (t,y) space; rendered statically (no propagation).

4.name: Superposition at specified time
4.distractor-shape: Wave with vertices in (x,y) space; rendered statically as a candidate sum-wave.

6.name: Reflection at boundary, single time
6.distractor-shape: Wave with vertices in (x,y) space; rendered statically as a candidate combined wave inside the boundary.

choices.distractors: Array of WaveSpec, length must equal count - 1
choices.shuffle: default true. Seeded by hash of (waveA + params + count) so the same input always produces the same shuffle.
choices.response: files.choices is an array in DISPLAY order. Each entry has {path|dataUrl, isCorrect, label, originalIndex}. originalIndex 0 is the correct answer in pre-shuffle order.

output.default: Files written to ./api_output/{sessionId}/. Response includes absolute paths.
output.outputDir: Override the directory (must be an absolute path).
output.inline: If true, response contains base64 dataUrls instead of file paths. No files are written.
output.manifest.json: Saved alongside files; contains the full request + response (for reproducibility).
